topic.what: Membership inference attacks on federated learning for hardware assurance
topic.how: Uses standard cell library layouts to guide gradient inversion attacks without auxiliary data, exploiting reconstruction fidelity differences
topic.why_matters: Reveals critical privacy vulnerability in FL for IC supply chain security; enables hardware-specific metadata inference (node types, layers) that accelerates reverse engineering, IP piracy, and physical attacks

characteristics.vs_baseline: first data-free MIA achieving >0.88 AUC without auxiliary data
characteristics.evidence: Table II and IV showing AUC 0.8868 (metal), 0.9804 (diffusion), 0.9916 (32nm)
characteristics.context: Two-client FL, 200 rounds, U-Net 16-layer, 141 synthetic SEM images

characteristics.what_itasures: Classification accuracy in distinguishing members from non-members
characteristics.evidence: Table II: 78% metal, 94% diffusion; Table IV: 93% 32nm, 69% 90nm
characteristics.context: Mean threshold attack using Dice similarity scores

characteristics.what_it_measures: Dice coefficient between reconstructed and target masks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Non-member reconstructions yield substantially lower scores (0.5876, 0.7130)
characteristics.evidence: Table I showing inter-layer Dice scores with matching vs mismatched SCLL guidance
characteristics.context: Post-processed binary masks from guided GIA reconstructions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational overhead for gradient inversion with dummy loss term
characteristics.vs_baseline: Standard GIA (λdummy=0) underperforms on complex structures
characteristics.evidence: Ablation study in Table III showing λdummy=5 optimal for metal (AUC 0.9752) and 32nm (AUC 0.9916)
characteristics.context: AMD EPYC ROMECPU + NVIDIA B200 GPU; 200 communication rounds

characteristics.what_it_measures: Inference of hardware metadata (node type, layer) vs generic data presence
characteristics.vs_baseline: Previous MIAs only detect data presence; DECIFR reveals underlying IC characteristics
characteristics.evidence: Authors state membership inference exposes hardware traits enabling reverse engineering and IP piracy
characteristics.context: Hardware assurance domain where IC characteristics are sensitive IP

characteristics.what_it_measures: Utility of countermeasures against GIA-based MIA
characteristics.vs_baseline: Trade-offs documented between privacy protection and model utility
characteristics.evidence: Table V assessing Differential Privacy, Synthetic Data, SMC, HE, and ZKP with specific benefits and drawbacks
characteristics.context: Domain-specific analysis for hardware assurance; no empirical benchmark results provided

novelty: first data-free MIA for FL segmentation without auxiliary datasets
SCLL-guided GIA using standard cell layouts as dummy labels
hardware-specific inference revealing technology nodes and layer types
negative Forward Pass loss term (Ldummy) to amplify reconstruction quality margins
post-processing bottleneck identification affecting complex structures
open_problems: refining evaluation metrics to reduce post-processing bottleneck
autonomous hyperparameter tuning for λdummy across diverse datasets
scalability to larger FL deployments with more clients
empirical benchmarking of defense mechanisms (DP, HE, SMC, ZKP)
assessment against complex backbones like Vision Transformers
privacy-utility trade-off quantification for perturbation methods
